steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what Drake's appreciation for Parisi's collection reveals about the two authors. Drake values the focus on 'the false starts...and mistakes that beset real scientific research every day.' — the messy human reality of science — and argues scientists must include this 'human stuff' to explain how science really works. The correct choice must reflect a shared value between them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It explores a subtle difference between Drake's and Parisi's respective objections to scientific research methods.’: invents a disagreement between Drake and Parisi; Drake's appreciation indicates agreement, not conflict. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It outlines an area of agreement between Parisi and Drake in how they think about the work of science.’: both authors value showing the human, imperfect side of scientific discovery — Parisi by collecting such stories, Drake by championing that approach. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘It describes Drake's view that nonfiction narrative is an effective way for Parisi to share his accomplishments.’: confuses the form or genre of their writing with a shared value; the passage is about content, not style. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It presents Drake's claim that Parisi is too modest about his own achievements as a scientist.’: misreads what the passage discusses; neither author's position is about the topic described here. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
